$comment: c76 (D-FF3-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-STACK-OVERFLOW-OFFSET, interleaved variant; 2026-07-01): the same SysV shared-cursor invariant as mixed_class_stack_overflow but with int and double params INTERLEAVED in the signature, so the per-class overflow cursors advance in interleaved order and must still resolve to distinct monotonic incoming-stack offsets. WITNESS: int probe(int i1, double d1, ..., int i7, double d7, double d8, double d9) — 7 ints (i7 overflows 6 GPR regs) + 9 doubles (d9 overflows 8 FP regs), both position-weighted with descending values so any slot collision/swap/wrong-offset changes the exit code. isum = sum(8-k)*k for k=1..7 = 84; dsum = sum(10-k)*k for k=1..9 = 165; total 249. FP args from a VOLATILE global (`one`==1) so the release arm does not trip D-OPT-RELEASE-FP-CONSTANT-UNLOADED-XMM (c77). RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the D-FF3 offset fix -> on SysV the FP overflow reads a GPR-overflow slot -> a wrong (not 249) value; AAPCS64/Win64 unaffected. => 249.
